coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Saturday long run, taper week 21: target 90–100 min at Z2 (140–150 bpm), rolling terrain with 800–1200 ft vert. You're 62 days out and volume is correctly suppressed (17.2 mi YTD vs. 30.2 last week), but your resting HR is elevated 3.2 bpm above 30-day average—likely travel fatigue or a deeper training effect. Keep this aerobic-only; no tempo pushes. The Julian Alps demand vert durability over speed right now, and a clean, easy long run sharpens your aerobic system without blunting recovery. Your 10.66h sleep last night is a strong foundation—use it.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Front-load 90–120g carbs 2 hours pre-run; carry 40–50g during (sports drink or gels at 45-min mark). Post-run, aim for 40–50g carbs + 20–25g protein within 30 min—you're in taper, not peak build, so recovery nutrition matters more than volume. Sparse aid stations in Slovenia mean you'll rely on self-supported fueling for long efforts; use today's 90-min run to dial in your bottle/gel strategy in the terrain you'll face. Hydration: 16–20 oz per hour in cool conditions.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Recovery score is solid at 74/100, but resting HR elevation and absence of HRV data suggest your nervous system is still processing travel load. Prioritize 8+ hours sleep tonight (you're averaging 7.7h); one night of 10.66h doesn't offset a chronic shortfall. Ice bath or 12-min cold plunge post-run if you have access—this is peak taper week for parasympathetic reset. Avoid any hard intensity tomorrow; active recovery or full rest day is non-negotiable.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're in week 21 of a 22-week cycle with 62 days to the Alps. The taper can feel anticlimactic—your brain is still hungry for intensity after months of Build and Peak. Resist it. This run is a nervous-system rehearsal, not a fitness opportunity. Use it to visualize: technical alpine descents, pacing discipline on 2500m vert, managing effort at sparse aid stations. The race is won in the taper; trust the work you've banked and let your body absorb it.
